meta.title: Gelson Marcelo — Mobile Developer
meta.description: Gelson Marcelo — mobile developer. Published apps: Ponto Maré, Coleta Frai, and Slice Heads.

hero.title: Hi, I'm Gelson Marcelo
hero.lead: I build Android apps. I work on mobile at Letras and, in my spare time, I publish my own apps focused on simple, useful experiences.

about.title: About
about.p1: I graduated in Systems Analysis and Development from Instituto Federal Catarinense and continue specializing in mobile development, with a focus on Android. I like learning by doing and turning ideas into apps published on the store.
about.p2: Beyond work projects, I maintain my own products — like the apps below — and their documentation on this site. I'm not an expert in everything; I prefer to deliver something clear, stable, and meaningful for those who use it.

projects.title: Published projects
projects.intro: Apps on Google Play and Windows, with documentation and privacy policy hosted here.

pontoMare.name: Ponto Maré
pontoMare.description: Point-of-sale system for stores: products, inventory, sales, cash register, payments, and receipt and label printing. Runs on Android and Windows desktop with the same account.

coletaFrai.name: Coleta Frai
coletaFrai.description: Environmental awareness and selective collection in Fraiburgo/SC: reminders, neighborhood schedules, drop-off points, and educational content.

contact.title: Contact

meta.title: Documentation — Coleta Frai
meta.description: Coleta Frai app documentation — reminders, schedules, and selective collection in Fraiburgo/SC.

coletaFraiDoc.title: Coleta Frai Documentation ♻️
coletaFraiDoc.intro: Coleta Frai aims to promote environmental awareness and facilitate selective waste collection in Fraiburgo/SC. In this documentation, you will find detailed information on how to use all Coleta Frai features:

notice.heading: Notice
notice.body: Notice: This app is a private initiative and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or linked to the City of Fraiburgo, Sanefrai, or any government agency. The information presented is for informational purposes only, obtained from public sources such as the Sanefrai website.

demo.heading: App demo
demo.intro: The video below provides an overview of Coleta Frai in use — screens, flows, and app features. Some segments show features that existed in development or prototype versions and may not be in the version published on Google Play.
demo.fallback: Your browser does not support video playback. You can download the MP4 file.
demo.caption: Coleta Frai overview demo.

lembretes.heading: Reminders
lembretes.body: Scheduling reminders is one of Coleta Frai's main tools. Remember that each neighborhood has its own recyclable collection schedule, and collection happens on specific days and times. Setting reminders helps users put recyclables out on the correct day and period so material is collected and processed properly. The collection truck only passes on the day and time set in each neighborhood's schedule; if material is not in the recyclable bag at that moment, it will not be picked up by selective collection and may end up in a landfill, which harms the environment.

cronograma.heading: Selective Collection Schedule/Route
cronograma.body: Choosing your neighborhood in Coleta Frai is essential so users access the correct selective collection schedule. This helps avoid confusion when disposing of recyclables and ensures collection on the right day. Users can easily check when the selective collection truck will pass in their neighborhood and plan to put recyclables out at the right time. The truck does not collect on days outside the schedule; wrong-day disposal can send material to a landfill and harm the environment. With Coleta Frai, users can do their part for the planet and support more efficient, sustainable selective collection.

rastreamento.heading: Truck tracking
rastreamento.p1: A selective collection truck tracking feature was developed and implemented, functional in production use, so users know where the vehicle is, whether it is near their home, or whether it has already passed — helping put recyclables on the street at the right time.
rastreamento.p2: The feature depends on a phone responsible for sending the truck's location (on the vehicle or with the collection crew) and other app users who view that position in real time on the map.
rastreamento.p3: Although ready and operational, this feature was not released in the Google Play version: the collection company chose not to proceed due to internal operational constraints — not because of a technical failure. In the app demo video you can see Coleta Frai in use, including segments where tracking appears.

dados.heading: Selective Collection Data in Fraiburgo/SC
dados.body: The feature that lets users view selective collection and recycling data for the municipality in charts is useful for those who want to follow trends in relevant data. These figures may not be up to date, as they depend on information from responsible agencies. Still, it gives a general picture of recycling in the city.

noticias.heading: News portal
noticias.body: One Coleta Frai feature is viewing news related to the environment and selective collection actions in Fraiburgo. News comes directly from the official SANEFRAI site (https://www.sanefrai.sc.gov.br/noticias), the agency responsible for solid waste management in the city.

pontos.heading: Voluntary Drop-off Points
pontos.body: Viewing voluntary drop-off points in Coleta Frai is a great way to find places to dispose of recyclables. The platform shows collection points across the city with contact details, location, and accepted materials. Users can also see points on a map to find the nearest one — including materials not collected by the selective collection truck.

instrucoes.heading: Selective Collection Instructions
instrucoes.body: Coleta Frai includes an explanatory video from the Green Bag campaign to guide residents on the importance of selective collection and how to sort recyclables correctly. It is especially useful for those still unsure and who want to contribute to a more sustainable world.

lixeiras.heading: Recycling bins and their colors
lixeiras.body: In the Green Bag program, dry recyclables can go together in the same bag, except organic waste, which selective collection does not pick up and should not go in the Green Bag. Coleta Frai explains recycling bin colors and which materials belong in each. Users can sort correctly at home, keep recyclables out of landfills, and support recycling — for a more sustainable city.

razoes.heading: Reasons to recycle and Recycling tips
razoes.intro: The "Reasons to Recycle" and "Recycling Tips" pages are important because they provide relevant information to raise awareness and teach users how to recycle properly.
razoes.razoesBody: On "Reasons to Recycle", users find convincing arguments on how recycling preserves the environment, reduces landfill waste, saves natural resources, and more — motivating more sustainable daily habits.
razoes.dicasBody: On "Recycling Tips", users find practical advice to support selective collection, avoid contaminated materials, and reduce waste.

darkTheme.heading: Dark theme
darkTheme.body: You can choose between light and dark themes in the app!

meta.title: Privacy policy — Coleta Frai
meta.description: Privacy policy and terms of use for the Coleta Frai app.

coletaFraiPrivacy.title: Privacy Policy and Terms of Use — Coleta Frai
coletaFraiPrivacy.intro1: This policy is maintained by Gelson Marcelo, developer of the Coleta Frai app, available on Google Play.
coletaFraiPrivacy.intro2: Thank you for choosing Coleta Frai. This privacy policy and terms of use ("Policy") describes how we collect, use, share, and protect your personal information when using the app. By using Coleta Frai, you agree to the terms below. If you do not agree, please do not use the app.

coleta.heading: Information Collection
coleta.p1: The app does not collect personally identifiable information such as name, email, or contacts. We may collect device information such as model, operating system, version, IP address, and unique identifiers.
coleta.notice: Notice: specific users cannot be identified from this data.

uso.heading: Use of Information
uso.body: Collected information is used to improve app operation, personalize the user experience, send notifications, and respond to support requests.

compartilhamento.heading: Information Sharing
compartilhamento.body: We do not share information with third parties except when required by law.

termos.heading: Terms of Use
termos.p1: Coleta Frai is an informational app and should not be used as the sole basis for important decisions.
termos.p2: By using the app, you agree not to:
items: Violate applicable laws or regulations;
Share false or misleading information from the app;
Interfere with or harm app operation;
Transmit harmful, threatening, abusive, defamatory, or illegal content.
termos.p3: We reserve the right to modify this policy at any time without prior notice. We recommend checking this page regularly for changes.

rows.details: Required to schedule exact alarms on devices with Android 12 (API 31) or higher. Used to ensure reminders configured by the user fire on time.
Required so the app can fire alarms exactly at the scheduled time. Essential for the main feature: reminding users about selective collection on specific days and times.
Required to load updated data on schedules, collection points, notifications, and to access the SANEFRAI portal.
Allows the app to restore active alarms after the device restarts.

contato.heading: Contact
contato.body: For privacy-related questions or requests, contact: gelsonschikorski1998@gmail.com.

meta.title: Documentation — Slice Heads
meta.description: Slice Heads Android game documentation — rules, Play Games, and app features.

sliceHeadsDoc.title: Slice Heads Documentation
sliceHeadsDoc.intro: Slice Heads is a casual Android game where you slice heads with swipe gestures, avoid bombs, and try to beat your high score. Gameplay uses interactive Rive animation (16:9, landscape) and optional Google Play Games for leaderboards and achievements.
sliceHeadsDoc.intro2: This documentation is a guide to app features, aligned with what is available in the menu and in-app screens.
sliceHeadsDoc.img1Alt: Landscape gameplay — slicing heads and score on HUD
sliceHeadsDoc.img2Alt: Gameplay with bomb on screen and strike indicators on HUD

notice.heading: Notice
notice.body: This app is an independent game developed by Gelson Schikorski. It is not affiliated with Rive, Inc. or the original marketplace animation author, beyond use of Heads Will Roll under the CC BY 4.0 license. Base animation: Heads Will Roll — Rive Marketplace.

menu.heading: Main menu
items: Play — start a match;
see Best Score (best score saved on device);
open Settings (sound settings);
read How to play;
view Credits (app and animation attributions);
open Privacy Policy (privacy policy in WebView);
sign in to Google Play Games (optional), open Leaderboards and Achievements when signed in.
menu.outro: Global leaderboard and achievements require Google account sign-in via Play Games.

gameplay.heading: How to play (match)
gameplay.p1: The main experience is in landscape because the animation is designed for 16:9. On phones, the game may ask you to rotate the device before starting.
gameplay.pause: During a match there is a pause button. When paused, animation and background music stop until you resume or return to the menu.

strikes.heading: Strikes and “Clear strikes” credits
strikes.intro: When you miss heads, you accumulate strikes. Optionally you can:
steps: In the pause menu, watch a rewarded video (ad) to earn a credit to clear strikes;
During a match, tap the Clear strikes button on the HUD (icon with badge when credits are available);
The button is dimmed when there are no credits; with credits, the badge shows how many you can use.
strikes.optional: Credits are optional — you can play without watching ads.
strikes.howToPlayNote: The in-app How to play screen shows a visual preview of this button (with and without credit).

sound.heading: Sound settings
sound.intro: In Settings (bottom sheet on the menu) you adjust:
items: background music volume;
mute music.
sound.outro: Music changes with match progress (tracks by score range) and there is specific music on game over. Music pauses during rewarded videos and when the game is paused.

playGames.heading: Google Play Games
items: Sign in — Google account login;
Leaderboards — submit best score to the game's configured leaderboard;
Achievements — unlock and track achievements during play.
playGames.outro: Without sign-in, Play Games leaderboard and achievements are unavailable; local best score is still saved on device.

inAppScreens.heading: How to play and Credits (in app)
inAppScreens.intro: Native in-app screens (no internet required except tappable links in credits):
items: How to play — detailed rules: gestures, bombs, strikes, difficulty, landscape, and clear strikes button demo;
Credits — thanks, Android app development info, and Heads Will Roll / isaganttus attribution with links to Rive marketplace and CC BY 4.0 license.

privacyInApp.heading: Privacy policy (in app)
privacyInApp.body: The policy is shown in a WebView from a URL configured by the developer. It should describe data use by services such as Firebase (analytics, crashes, performance), Google AdMob (ads), and Play Games, per the published version.

localData.heading: Data saved on device
localData.intro: Without a Slice Heads account, the app may store locally:
items: best score;
music volume and mute;
internal flags for achievements already processed.
localData.backup: With Android automatic backup enabled, this data may be included in system backup per manufacturer settings.

requirements.heading: Requirements and permissions
items: Android 8.0+ (API 26, minSdk 26);
Internet permission (privacy policy, ads, Play Games, Firebase);
landscape play recommended on phones.

animationCredits.heading: Animation credits
animationCredits.body: Gameplay is based on Heads Will Roll by isaganttus, adapted for Slice Heads, under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

footer.heading: Contact and feedback
footer.note: Slice Heads — user documentation · last revision aligned with app v1.0

meta.title: Privacy policy — Slice Heads
meta.description: Privacy policy for the Slice Heads Android game.

sliceHeadsPrivacy.title: Privacy Policy — Slice Heads
sliceHeadsPrivacy.intro1: This Privacy Policy describes how the Slice Heads mobile application ("App", "we") handles information when you install or use the App on Android devices distributed via Google Play.
sliceHeadsPrivacy.consent: By using the App, you agree to this Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the App.

s1.heading: 1. Overview
s1.intro: Slice Heads is a casual game. To function, the App may:
items: store preferences and progress locally on your device;
use Google services for analytics, performance, stability, and ads;
display ads, including optional rewarded videos;
connect to Google Play Games for leaderboards and achievements if you choose to use those features.
s1.noSale: The developer does not sell personal data.
s1.thirdParty: Although the App does not use its own server for user accounts, third-party services integrated into the App — such as Google Firebase, Google AdMob, and Google Play Games — may process certain technical data and identifiers under their own privacy policies.

s2.heading: 2. Data we collect and how we use it

s21.heading: 2.1 Data stored locally on device
s21.intro: The App uses Android DataStore (Preferences) to store information directly on the user's device, including:
s21.p1: This data stays on the user's device and is not sent to the developer's own servers.
s21.p2: Android may also back up App data automatically per your Google account and device manufacturer settings.
s21.legalBasis: Legal basis (LGPD): contract performance and legitimate interest for proper game operation.

s22.heading: 2.2 Google Play Games (optional)
s22.p1: If you use features such as sign-in, leaderboards, or achievements, the App uses Google Play Games.
s22.p2: In that case, Google may process data associated with your Google account, including:
items: authenticated account identifiers;
scores submitted to the game leaderboard;
achievement progress.
s22.optional: Play Games is optional. You can manage data and permissions in your Google account settings.

s23.heading: 2.3 Firebase (Google)
s23.intro: The App integrates Firebase services provided by Google.

analytics.heading: Firebase Analytics
analytics.intro: The App may log usage events such as:
items: match start and end;
new records;
rewarded ad interaction;
Play Games sign-in success or failure.
analytics.outro: These events help understand App usage and improve user experience.

crashlytics.heading: Firebase Crashlytics
crashlytics.intro: If the App crashes, technical reports may be sent automatically, including:
items: stack traces;
device model;
Android version;
App version.

performance.heading: Firebase Performance Monitoring
performance.body: The App may collect metrics related to application performance and stability.

s24.heading: 2.4 Google AdMob (ads)
s24.p1: The App uses the Google Mobile Ads SDK (AdMob) to display ads.
adTypes: rewarded video ads shown only when the user chooses to watch for in-game benefits.
s24.p3: Google and its advertising partners may process certain technical data, including:
s24.p4: This data may be used for:
s24.optOut: You can reset or limit the advertising identifier in Android settings.

s25.heading: 2.5 Data we do not collect directly
s25.intro: The App does not request access to:
items: precise or approximate location;
camera;
microphone;
contacts;
SMS;
user personal files;
financial data;
health data.
s25.outro: Slice Heads also has no proprietary sign-up system with username and password.

s3.heading: 3. Sharing with third parties
s3.intro: The App uses third-party services for operation, including:

tableRows.thirdParty: Google LLC (Firebase, AdMob, Play Games, Play Store)
tableRows.purpose: Analytics, ads, stability, social features, and App distribution

s3.noSale: We do not sell personal data.
s3.legal: Information may be shared when required by law or to protect the security, rights, and integrity of the App and its users.

s4.heading: 4. International transfers
s4.p1: Services used by the App may process data on servers outside the user's country, including the United States.
s4.p2: For users in Brazil, data processing follows the LGPD. In other regions, applicable local laws may be observed by respective service providers.

tableRows.retention: Until manual removal, data clearing, or uninstallation
For the period needed to handle the request

s6.heading: 6. Security
s6.intro: The App uses reasonable measures to protect information, including:
items: secure communication via HTTPS/TLS;
standard Android local storage;
data minimization in analytics events.
s6.disclaimer: No storage or transmission method is completely secure.

s7.heading: 7. Your rights
s7.intro: Depending on applicable law, you may request:
items: confirmation of processing;
access to data;
correction of information;
deletion of data;
withdrawal of consent, where applicable.
s7.googleManage: Data linked to your Google account, Play Games, or ad services can also be managed in your Google account:
s7.localRemovalPath: Android → Settings → Apps → Slice Heads → Storage → Clear data

s8.heading: 8. Children and teens
s8.p1: Slice Heads is intended for a general audience.
s8.p2: We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent.
s8.p3: If a legal guardian believes a child has provided personal data improperly, they may contact us using the email in this Policy.

s9.heading: 9. Links and external content
s9.body: This Policy may be made available inside the App via an external web page. Third-party sites and services have their own privacy policies.

s10.heading: 10. Changes to this Policy
s10.p1: This Policy may be updated periodically to reflect changes in the App, services used, or legal requirements.
s10.p2: The latest version will be available at the official Policy URL, with the update date.

s11.heading: 11. Applicable law
s11.body: This Policy is governed by the applicable laws of the Federative Republic of Brazil, without prejudice to rights under applicable data protection legislation for the user.

meta.title: Documentation — Ponto Maré
meta.description: Documentation for the Ponto Maré app.

pontoMareDoc.title: Ponto Maré Documentation
pontoMareDoc.intro: Ponto Maré is a point-of-sale system for stores, with features for product registration, inventory control, sales records, payments, cash register management, receipt printing, and other resources related to commercial operations.

privacy.heading: Privacy policy
privacy.link: Open the Ponto Maré privacy policy.

terms.heading: Terms of use
terms.link: Open the Ponto Maré terms of use.

pontoMareDoc.footerHtml: Home · Downloads · Privacy policy · Terms of use
contentHtml: Ponto Maré Documentation
Last updated: June 17, 2026

Android package: com.gelsonschikorski.pontomare
Reference version: 1.0.0+18
Contact: contato.pontomare@gmail.com

Ponto Maré is a point-of-sale system for stores. It was built to help with product registration, inventory control, sales records, cash register control, payment tracking, receipt and label printing, backups, and operational settings for the business.
This documentation provides an overview of the app and describes the main features available to the user.

Important Links

  • Downloads (Android and Windows)
  • Privacy policy
  • Terms of use
  • Email support
Notice
Ponto Maré is an independent app developed by Gelson Schikorski. It does not replace professional accounting, tax, legal, or administrative advice.
The user is responsible for checking registered data, product values, sales records, payments, inventory, cash register records, reports, and receipts generated by the app.
Part of the data may be stored locally on the device. For this reason, we recommend keeping backups up to date and protecting access to the device.
App Overview
Ponto Maré organizes store operations into modules accessed from the side menu:

• Cash Register: tracking and recording cash inflows and outflows.
  • Sale: the main point-of-sale screen where products are added and sales are completed.
  • Sales: sales history, details, receipt printing, and undo sale.
  • Products: creation, editing, search, inventory, prices, costs, colors, and label printing.
  • Payments: listing and filtering registered payments.
  • Settings: payment methods, payment terminals, theme, sound, printing, logo, backup, restore, subscription, account, and administrator password.
  • Remote Products: optional remote catalog to create or link local products and import quantities.
  • Logs: technical system records protected by administrator password.
Account, Login, and Access
To access Ponto Maré, the user uses an account with email and password.

On the initial screen, the user can sign in with an existing account, create a new account, switch between login and registration, enter email, password, and password confirmation, and view error messages when authentication fails.
Subscription and Trial Period

Ponto Maré has subscription-based access control. The subscription may be validated by services such as Google Play on Android and by an external flow on desktop platforms, depending on the available configuration.
When access is granted, the app opens normally. When the trial period ends or the subscription is not active, the app displays a screen explaining that a subscription is required.

On the subscription screen, the user can view the available plan, subscribe through the purchase flow, restore purchases when available, and sign out. The app may also show a warning when the trial period is close to ending.
Menu and Navigation
After login and access validation, the user enters the main app area.
The menu may appear as an expanded sidebar, compact bar, or drawer depending on screen size. This supports use on larger screens such as desktop/tablet and on smaller screens.
The menu also offers quick actions such as backing up the database, signing out, and navigating between modules.
Cash Register Module
The Cash Register module gathers the financial records for the day, including manual inflows, manual outflows, and sales.

On the cash register screen, the user can view records, select a date, move forward or back one day, clear the filter, filter sales by payment method, add manual inflows or outflows, view details, and delete records when allowed.
Manual cash register records
When creating or editing a manual record, the user enters the record type, amount, inflow/outflow indication, note, and date/time when the record already exists.
These records are useful for noting movements that did not come directly from a sale, such as cash reinforcement, withdrawal, expense, cash removal, or adjustment.
Cash register day rollover time
In settings, the user can define the cash register rollover time. This time determines when records start being considered part of the next day.
For example, if set to 02:00, when viewing the cash register for 12/30, the system considers records from 02:00 on 12/30 until 01:59 on 12/31. This is useful for stores that close after midnight.
Sale Module

The Sale module is the main POS screen. In it, the user can enter or scan a product code, search products manually, add products to the sale, view items, see product details, remove units, follow the total in real time, delete the sale in progress, and complete the sale.
Sale in progress rules
The sale in progress is kept by the app until it is completed or deleted. This helps recover the flow if the app is closed during an operation.
Products without inventory cannot be added to the sale. When a sale is completed, inventory and financial records are updated according to the products and payments entered.
Product search during a sale
When the operator does not know the product code, they can open product search to find registered products and choose an item to add to the sale.
Completing a sale

When completing a sale, the app opens the payment dialog. At this step, the user can review subtotal, discount, total, and paid amount; apply a discount; add payments; define payment amounts; select payment method; select a payment terminal when required; add notes; choose receipt printing; and finish the sale.
When payments do not cover the sale total, the app shows a warning and asks for confirmation before completing the sale with insufficient payment.
Sales Module

The Sales module shows the history of registered sales. In the list, the user can view completed sales, open details, edit allowed information when the sale is still editable, print receipts, and undo a sale.
When opening a sale, the app shows the related products and payments. When the sale can still be edited, the user can adjust allowed details. Otherwise, the screen works as a read-only view.
Products Module
The Products module is used to create, edit, search, and control local store products.

On the products screen, the user can create a local product, search by text, show or hide out-of-stock products, view products in a table, print labels, edit products, and delete products with administrator password confirmation.
Product creation and editing
The product form includes fields such as code, quantity, name, price, cost, note, and colors.

The local code field does not necessarily represent the final full code typed by the user. The user enters or suggests only the editable part of the code; the app builds and validates the final code using the EAN-8 standard. When the product already exists, the code cannot be changed.
Local code, EAN-8, and barcode
Ponto Maré works with codes in the EAN-8 standard, which has 8 digits. This format makes it possible to generate codes compatible with barcode reading on labels.

• First digit: indicates the code range and helps the system distinguish local products from remote products.
  • Middle digits: represent the product numeric part that the user can enter or suggest.
  • Last digit: is the EAN-8 checksum, calculated automatically by the app.

If the user leaves the code blank, the system automatically generates an available local code. If the user enters part of the code, it is treated as a suggestion to form a valid EAN-8. The last digit should not be entered manually.
Inventory, price, cost, and colors

Inventory is controlled by the registered quantity and by movements made through sales or imports. A product has sale price and cost, and the cost can be protected by administrator password to avoid exposing sensitive margin or purchase information.
A product can also be associated with registered colors. The color selector allows viewing existing colors, selecting colors for the product, creating new colors, and using a visual color picker.
Labels
Products can be printed as labels. When creating a new product, the user can choose to print a label after saving. For existing products, the print action is available directly on the product.
Payments Module
The Payments module lists payments registered in sales. It allows filtering payments by payment method, payment terminal, start date, and end date.
These filters help verify amounts received by period, payment type, and device. When selecting a payment, the user can open the related sale to check details.
Payment Methods
In settings, the user can manage payment methods. The app already creates some default methods, such as Cash, PIX, Debit, Credit upfront, and Installment credit.
Each payment method can indicate whether it requires a payment terminal. The user can select, create, edit, or delete methods when allowed, and define the default payment method for new sales.
Payment Terminals and Devices
The app allows registering devices or payment terminals to associate with payments. Each device has a name, note, and active/inactive status.
The user can create, edit, or delete a payment terminal when possible, and define a default terminal. When a payment method requires a device, terminal selection appears in the payment flow.
Default Payment

In settings, it is possible to define a default payment method and default payment terminal. These options are used automatically when completing a sale, making the flow faster when the store usually uses the same method or terminal.
Remote Products

The Remote Products module is an optional feature for working with a remote catalog linked to the user's account. This screen may be hidden when the feature is disabled in the app remote configuration.

This module can be used to create and update remote products, search products, show or hide archived and out-of-stock products, create local products from remote products, link remote products to local products, import pending quantities, run batch actions, and archive or restore products.
Difference between local and remote products

Local product is stored in the device database and used directly in sales. Remote product is stored on a server, associated with the user's account, and can be used as a source to create a local product, link to a local product, or import pending quantity.
Example use away from the store
A common use case for remote products is registering products away from the store. A shop owner can register products on another device before bringing the merchandise to the store's main system.

1. The shop owner is away from the store, for example at home, in storage, or at a supplier.
  2. They register products as remote products on a secondary device.
  3. Each remote product already has name, code, price, cost, note, and pending quantity.
  4. When the merchandise reaches the store, the user opens Ponto Maré on the main device.
  5. On the Remote Products screen, they import the products into the local database.
  6. After import, products are ready for sale in the local store system.
This flow helps separate registration work from checkout service and reduces the chance of physical products remaining in the store without registration, labels, or defined prices.
Batch actions and linking assistant

When selecting multiple remote products, the app may show a batch actions bar. Unlinked products can be created as local products; linked products with pending quantity can have quantities imported; non-applicable products are ignored for that action.

When the app needs to link a remote product to a local product, it may show an assistant to compare information and resolve differences between remote and local data, such as name, price, cost, note, or code.
Printing

Ponto Maré includes printing features for receipts and labels. Printing settings allow choosing print method, printer, paper size, print type, quantity, PDF preview, label measurements, receipt header and footer, logo use, and support for font type B in some ESC/POS flows.
Print methods and types
The app supports printing through Bluetooth, USB, System (PDF only), and LP command (Unix/Linux).
Available print types include PDF ESC/POS image-bytes, Raw ESC/POS bytes, and Raw TSPL bytes. The app allows selecting 58 mm, 72 mm, and 80 mm paper sizes.
Receipts and labels

For sales receipts, the user can configure header, footer, logo inclusion, printing after sale completion, and reprinting from history. For labels, the user can configure width, height, gap, quantity, and compatible print type.
Store Logo

In settings, the user can define a store logo. The logo can be used in the interface and in some print types depending on the selected configuration. The user can change, remove, and view the configured logo.
Theme, Appearance, and Sound Feedback
The app allows choosing the visual theme. Depending on the available configuration, the user can switch between light theme, dark theme, and system theme.
Ponto Maré can also play feedback sounds for success, error, and warning operations. In settings, the user can enable or disable this behavior.
Administrator Password

Some areas or actions are protected by administrator password. On first use, before any custom configuration, the default password is admin. We recommend changing this password in settings before allowing store operators to use the app.
The password may be required to delete products, access logs, clear logs, view sensitive information such as product cost, and delete the account.
Backups and Restore

Ponto Maré includes database backup and restore features. In settings, the user can view and update the last backup date, perform a manual backup, restore a backup, import a file, optimize databases, and share logs with developers.
Manual backup sends a copy of the data to the configured storage and reduces the risk of data loss in case of device replacement, device failure, accidental deletion, or reinstallation.
When restoring, we recommend checking whether the selected backup corresponds to the desired store and period, because restore may replace local data.
System Logs
The Logs module records technical events and important app actions. Access is protected by administrator password.
An authorized user can view paginated logs, filter by text, log type, and date, and clear logs with password confirmation. Log types include error, warning, information, debug, and action.
Subscription Status

In settings, the user can open the subscription status dialog. This screen may show subscription status, provider, start date, expiration date, remaining days, and whether app access is allowed or blocked.
Possible statuses include trial period, active, expired, and canceled.
Account Deletion
The user can access the Delete account option in settings. Before opening deletion confirmation, the app asks for the administrator password.

The deletion flow explains that the action is permanent, server data and the user account will be removed, an active subscription may be lost, local data may remain on the device, and to remove it the user must uninstall the app or clear Ponto Maré data in the operating system.
Data Saved on the Device

Ponto Maré uses a local database to store operational information, such as products, codes, colors, prices, costs, sales, sold items, payments, payment methods, payment terminals, cash register records, preferences, printing settings, store logo, and system logs.
This data stays on the device and may remain even after deleting the online account. To remove local data, the user must uninstall the app or clear Ponto Maré data in the operating system settings.
Remote Data and Online Services
Some features depend on online services such as Supabase, Firebase Analytics, Firebase Crashlytics, Firebase Remote Config, and Google Play Billing.

POS operational data is mainly local. Cloud resources are used for authentication, subscription, backups, remote products, and settings, but this does not mean all local sales and movements are synchronized in real time between devices.

Features that may depend on internet include login, registration, subscription validation, subscription purchase and restore, remote products, remote backup, backup restore, remote settings, and sending logs to support.
Android Permissions

On Android, Ponto Maré may request permissions such as Internet, Bluetooth, and approximate or precise location. Location permission may be required to search for nearby Bluetooth devices, but the app does not use it to track the user's location.
Permissions can be managed in the operating system settings.
Requirements and Compatibility
Ponto Maré is a Flutter app with primary support for Android and structure for other platforms depending on configured build and distribution.

• Android package: com.gelsonschikorski.pontomare
  • Minimum SDK: Android API 26
  • Target SDK: Android API 35
Printer compatibility depends on model, connection method, permissions, drivers, operating system, paper, supported commands, and selected print type.
Usage Recommendations

1. Register products, prices, and costs correctly.
  2. Check the total before completing sales.
  3. Use payment methods and terminals consistent with the real operation.
  4. Back up frequently.
  5. Protect the administrator password.
  6. Do not share the account with unauthorized people.
  7. Periodically check whether printing is working.
  8. Keep the app updated.
  9. Keep the device protected with password or biometrics.
Suggested Initial Flow for a Store

1. Create an account or sign in with an existing account.
  2. Check subscription or trial period status.
  3. Open settings.
  4. Register payment methods.
  5. Register payment terminals/devices.
  6. Set default payment.
  7. Configure theme and sound feedback.
  8. Configure printing.
  9. Add the store logo.
  10. Register products.
  11. Test label printing.
  12. Make a test sale.
  13. Test receipt printing.
  14. Check cash register, sales, and payments.
  15. Make the first backup.
Privacy Policy and Terms
For information about data collection, use, storage, sharing, and deletion, see the Ponto Maré Privacy Policy.
For usage rules, user responsibilities, subscription, limitations, and general conditions, see the Ponto Maré Terms of Use.

Contact and Feedback

Questions and support: contato.pontomare@gmail.com

Bugs: support channel to be defined.

Suggestions: ideas channel to be defined.

Ponto Maré - user documentation.

meta.title: Downloads — Ponto Maré
meta.description: Downloads and release history for Ponto Maré on Android and Windows.

pontoMareReleases.title: Downloads — Ponto Maré
pontoMareReleases.intro: Download Ponto Maré for Android on Google Play or install the desktop version for Windows. Use the same email and password account on all platforms — an active subscription works on any of them.

android.heading: Android
android.description: Available on Google Play for phones and tablets running Android 8.0 (API 26) or later.

windows.heading: Windows (desktop)
windows.description: Installer for Windows 10 or later (64-bit). Works with the same account and subscription as on Android.

subscription.heading: Subscription
subscription.crossPlatformNotice: Subscription per account: your subscription is linked to your Ponto Maré account, not to a single device. If you subscribe on Android (Google Play) or on Windows (Mercado Pago on desktop), access is granted on any platform where you sign in with the same email and password.
subscription.desktopNote: On Android, subscribe through Google Play. On desktop, open the subscription option inside the app — payment opens in the browser and must be started from the app so it is linked to your account. See Ponto Maré Premium for details.

install.heading: How to install on Windows
install.intro: Follow the steps below after downloading the installer. If Windows shows a security warning, that is common for apps that do not yet have a Microsoft-recognized code signing certificate.
steps: Download the .exe installer using the Download for Windows button on this page.
Open your Downloads folder (or the location chosen in the browser) and run the Ponto Maré setup file.
If Windows SmartScreen shows “Windows protected your PC”, click More info and then Run anyway.
Follow the setup wizard until installation completes. Only proceed if you downloaded the file from this official page.
Open Ponto Maré from the Start menu or desktop shortcut and sign in with your account (email and password).
On first launch, check subscription or trial status in the app settings.
install.smartScreenNotice: Windows SmartScreen notice: when opening the installer for the first time, Windows may show “Windows protected your PC”. Click More info and then Run anyway to continue. The installer is distributed by the Ponto Maré developer; make sure you downloaded it from this official page.

changelog.heading: Release history
changelog.intro: Main changes in each published version. Older versions remain available for download when listed below.
changelog.downloadWindows: Download Windows installer for this version
v1_0_0_18: Ponto Maré version 1.0.0 for store operations on Android and Windows.
Sales, products, inventory, cash register, payments, and operational settings modules.
Receipt and label printing, local and remote backup, remote products, and admin-protected logs.
Email and password account; subscription on Android or Windows applies to the same account on any platform.
Windows desktop version with the same core features as the Android app.

pontoMareReleases.footerHtml: Home · Documentation · Privacy policy · Terms of use

meta.title: Privacy policy — Ponto Maré
meta.description: Privacy policy for the Ponto Maré app.

pontoMarePrivacy.title: Privacy Policy — Ponto Maré
pontoMarePrivacy.intro1: This Privacy Policy describes how the Ponto Maré app collects, uses, stores, shares, and protects information when you use the app.
pontoMarePrivacy.intro2: Ponto Maré is a point-of-sale system for stores, designed to help with product registration, inventory control, sales records, payments, cash register management, receipt printing, and related commercial operation features.
pontoMarePrivacy.intro3: By using Ponto Maré, you agree to the practices described in this Privacy Policy.

s1.heading: 1. Data controller
s1.p1: Ponto Maré is provided by Gelson Schikorski.
s1.p2: For questions, privacy requests, or requests related to your personal data, contact us by email:

s2.heading: 2. Information we may collect
s2.intro: Ponto Maré may collect or store the following information, depending on the features used by the user:
items: Account information, such as email, user identifier, and password encrypted/managed by the authentication provider.
Authentication and session information required for login, account security, and access control.

Operational data registered in the app, such as products, codes, prices, inventory, colors, sales, payments, payment methods, devices/payment terminals, cash register movements, employees, and information related to store management.
Subscription, purchase, and payment status data when the user uses plans, subscriptions, or purchases processed by Google Play.
Technical device and app data, such as app version, operating system, technical identifiers, crash logs, usage events, and information needed for diagnostics, security, and service improvement.
Backup and restore files when the user uses backup or sync features.
Information required for printing, pairing, or communicating with Bluetooth or USB printers, or printing resources available on the device.
s2.sensitive: The app is not intended to collect sensitive data, such as health data, religion, political opinions, racial or ethnic origin, unless the user improperly enters this type of information in free-text fields.

s3.heading: 3. How we use information
s3.intro: Information collected or stored by Ponto Maré is used to:
items: Create, authenticate, and protect user accounts.
Enable point-of-sale, inventory, sales, cash register, payment, and printing features.
Save data locally on the device and, where applicable, on servers for backup, sync, authentication, or remote features.
Validate subscriptions, purchases, and access rights through Google Play.
Improve app stability, performance, security, and user experience.
Identify and fix errors, crashes, and technical failures.
Comply with legal or regulatory obligations, or valid requests from competent authorities, where applicable.

s4.heading: 4. Local storage on the device
s4.p1: Part of Ponto Maré data is stored locally on the user's device, including store operational data such as products, sales, cash register records, payments, and settings.
s4.p2: This local data remains on the device even if the online account is deleted, unless the operating system or the user removes the app data. To also delete local data, the user can uninstall the app or clear Ponto Maré data in the device operating system settings.
s4.p3: If the same device is shared by more than one person, other accounts or users with access to the device may view local data already stored in the app.

s5.heading: 5. Third-party services
s5.intro: Ponto Maré uses third-party services to provide, protect, and improve its features. These services may collect and process data according to their own privacy policies:
items: Supabase: authentication, database, storage, backup, and resources related to accounts and remote data.
Firebase/Google: Firebase Analytics, Firebase Crashlytics, and Firebase Remote Config, used for usage analytics, crash diagnostics, remote configuration, and app improvement.
Google Play: processing, validation, and management of purchases and subscriptions made by the user.
s5.recommendation: We recommend that you review the privacy policies of these services to understand how they handle personal data.

s6.heading: 6. Information sharing
s6.noSale: Ponto Maré does not sell users' personal data.
s6.intro: We may share information only in the following situations:
items: With technology providers required for the app to function, such as Supabase, Firebase/Google, and Google Play.
When necessary for authentication, backup, sync, subscription validation, crash diagnostics, or security.
When required by law, court order, request from a competent authority, or to protect the rights, security, and integrity of the service.
With the user's own authorization.

s7.heading: 7. App permissions
s7.intro: Ponto Maré may request device permissions to enable specific features:
items: Internet: required for login, authentication, backup, remote configuration, subscriptions, purchases, and communication with online services.
Bluetooth: required to find, pair, and communicate with compatible printers or devices.

Approximate or precise location: in some Android versions, location permission may be required to search for and connect to nearby Bluetooth devices. Ponto Maré does not use this permission to track the user's location.
Files, images, or storage: where available, may be used to select, import, export, generate, or restore files related to app functionality.
s7.manage: The user can manage permissions in the operating system settings. Revoking permissions may limit or prevent some features from working.

s8.heading: 8. Purchases and subscriptions
s8.p1: Ponto Maré may offer subscriptions or purchases processed by Google Play.
s8.p2: Payment information is processed by Google Play according to Google's policies and terms. Ponto Maré does not store full credit card details or other sensitive financial data from the user.
s8.p3: We may store information related to subscription status, subscription identifiers, validity period, payment provider, and data needed to enable or restrict features according to the subscribed plan.

s9.heading: 9. Data security
s9.p1: We adopt reasonable security measures to protect data against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or improper destruction.
s9.p2: Even so, no method of internet transmission or electronic storage is completely secure. Therefore, absolute security cannot be guaranteed.
s9.p3: The user is also responsible for keeping the device, account, password, and physical access to the app secure.

s10.heading: 10. Data retention and deletion
s10.p1: We retain personal and operational data for as long as necessary to provide the service, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, maintain security, perform backups, or respond to user requests.
s10.p2: The user may request account deletion in the app when the feature is available. Account deletion removes data stored on servers related to the user's account, according to the service implementation and applicable technical or legal limitations.
s10.p3: Data stored locally on the device is not necessarily removed by deleting the online account. To remove local data, the user must uninstall the app or clear Ponto Maré data in the device operating system settings.
s10.p4: Backups, technical records, and logs may be retained for a limited period when necessary for security, fraud prevention, problem diagnosis, legal compliance, or service integrity.

s11.heading: 11. User rights
s11.intro: Under applicable law, including the Brazilian General Personal Data Protection Law (LGPD), the user may have the right to:
items: Confirm whether personal data is being processed.
Access personal data associated with their account.
Request correction of incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated data.
Request deletion, blocking, or anonymization of personal data, where applicable.
Request information about data sharing.
Withdraw consent when processing depends on consent.
s11.contact: To exercise these rights, contact us using the email provided in this policy.

s12.heading: 12. Children's privacy
s12.p1: Ponto Maré is intended for use by stores, businesses, professionals, and people responsible for commercial operations. The app is not directed to children.
s12.p2: We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has provided personal data through the app, contact us so we can evaluate and take appropriate action.

s13.heading: 13. International transfer and processing
s13.body: Some services used by Ponto Maré may process or store data on servers located outside Brazil. By using the app, you acknowledge that your data may be processed in other countries, subject to applicable protection measures and the policies of the providers used.

s14.heading: 14. Changes to this policy
s14.p1: This Privacy Policy may be updated periodically to reflect changes in the app, services used, legal requirements, or privacy practices.
s14.p2: When relevant changes occur, the update date will be changed. We recommend that users review this policy regularly.

s15.heading: 15. Contact
s15.intro: For questions about this Privacy Policy or about data processing by Ponto Maré, contact us:

pontoMarePrivacy.footerHtml: Home · Ponto Maré documentation · Terms of use

meta.title: Terms of use — Ponto Maré
meta.description: Terms of use for the Ponto Maré app.

pontoMareTerms.title: Ponto Maré Terms of Use
pontoMareTerms.intro1: These Terms of Use govern access to and use of the Ponto Maré app.
pontoMareTerms.intro2: Ponto Maré is a point-of-sale system for stores, designed to help with product registration, inventory control, sales records, payments, cash register management, receipt printing, and related commercial operation features.
pontoMareTerms.intro3: By accessing or using Ponto Maré, you state that you have read, understood, and agree to these Terms of Use and to the Ponto Maré Privacy Policy.

s1.heading: 1. App owner
s1.p1: Ponto Maré is provided by Gelson Schikorski.
s1.p2: For questions, support, or requests related to these Terms of Use, contact us by email:

s2.heading: 2. Acceptance of terms
s2.p1: By creating an account, accessing, installing, or using Ponto Maré, you agree to comply with these Terms of Use.
s2.p2: If you do not agree with these terms, do not use the app.
s2.p3: Use of the app is also subject to the Ponto Maré Privacy Policy, which explains how data is collected, used, stored, and shared.

s3.heading: 3. License to use
s3.p1: Ponto Maré grants the user a limited, personal, revocable, non-exclusive, and non-transferable license to use the app according to these Terms of Use.
s3.p2: The user does not acquire ownership of the app, source code, brand, visual identity, screens, features, documentation, or any other element related to Ponto Maré.
s3.p3: It is prohibited to copy, modify, distribute, sell, sublicense, rent, reverse engineer, decompile, attempt to extract the source code, or commercially exploit the app without express authorization.

s4.heading: 4. User account
s4.p1: Some Ponto Maré features may require account creation and authentication by email and password.
items: Providing accurate and up-to-date information.
Keeping the password confidential.
Protecting access to the device used with the app.
Immediately reporting any unauthorized account use.
s4.p2: Activities performed from the user's account or device may be considered the user's own responsibility.

s5.heading: 5. Permitted use
s5.p1: The user must use Ponto Maré only for lawful purposes compatible with its point-of-sale system proposal.
s5.intro: It is prohibited to use the app to:
items: Violate laws, regulations, or third-party rights.
Enter false, illegal, offensive data or data that violates third-party rights.
Attempt to access accounts, data, servers, or systems without authorization.
Interfere with the operation of the app, servers, or third-party services.
Bypass subscription, payment, access, or security controls.
Use the app in a way that may harm other users, the service, or its providers.

s6.heading: 6. Data registered by the user
s6.p1: The user is responsible for data registered in Ponto Maré, including products, prices, inventory, sales, payments, cash register movements, employees, devices, settings, and other store operational information.
s6.p2: Ponto Maré is a tool to support commercial management. The user is responsible for checking the accuracy of entered information, generated records, charged amounts, reports, receipts, and any decision made based on app data.
s6.p3: The app does not replace professional accounting, tax, legal, or administrative advice.

s7.heading: 7. Local storage, backups, and restore
s7.p1: Part of Ponto Maré data may be stored locally on the user's device.
s7.p2: The user is responsible for keeping the device in good working condition, protecting access to the device, and performing backups when the feature is available.
s7.p3: Backup, sync, or restore features may depend on internet access, authentication, available storage space, third-party services, server availability, and app settings.
s7.p4: Although measures are adopted to preserve data integrity, it is not possible to guarantee that local data, backups, or restores will always be available, complete, or error-free.
s7.p5: We recommend that users keep backup copies and regularly check whether important data has been saved correctly.

s8.heading: 8. Printers, devices, and integrations
s8.p1: Ponto Maré may offer printing features and communication with devices such as Bluetooth printers, USB printers, operating system printers, or other compatible equipment.
s8.p2: These features may depend on system permissions, equipment compatibility, drivers, pairing, connection, battery, network, cables, device settings, and third-party libraries.
s8.p3: Ponto Maré does not guarantee compatibility with all printer models, payment terminals, devices, operating system versions, or usage environments.

s9.heading: 9. Subscriptions, payments, and purchases
s9.p1: Ponto Maré may offer plans, subscriptions, trial periods, or purchases processed by Google Play.
s9.p2: Payments, renewals, cancellations, refunds, and payment methods are processed according to Google Play rules and the Google account used by the user.
s9.p3: Access to paid features may depend on purchase confirmation, subscription validation, internet connection, and availability of Google Play services.
s9.p4: Subscription cancellation must be performed by the user in Google Play, where applicable. Uninstalling the app or deleting the account in the app does not automatically cancel an active Google Play subscription.
s9.p5: When a subscription expires, is canceled, is declined, or cannot be validated, access to paid features may be limited, suspended, or terminated.

s10.heading: 10. Account deletion and ending use
s10.p1: The user may request or perform account deletion when the feature is available in the app.
s10.p2: Account deletion may remove data linked to the account on servers, as described in the Privacy Policy and according to applicable technical or legal limitations.
s10.p3: Data stored locally on the device may remain available even after deleting the online account. To remove local data, the user must uninstall the app or clear Ponto Maré data in the device operating system settings.
s10.p4: Account deletion may result in loss of access to subscriptions, backups, sync, remote data, and features linked to the account.

s11.heading: 11. App availability
s11.p1: We seek to keep Ponto Maré available and working correctly, but we do not guarantee that the app will be free from interruptions, errors, failures, data loss, incompatibilities, or unavailability.
s11.p2: Access to the app and to some features may depend on internet access, third-party services, servers, Google Play, Supabase, Firebase, the operating system, the device used, and permissions granted by the user.
s11.p3: Features may be modified, suspended, removed, or updated for bug fixes, improvements, security, maintenance, legal compliance, or product evolution.

s12.heading: 12. Limitation of liability
s12.p1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Ponto Maré and its owners will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, punitive damages, loss of profits, loss of revenue, loss of data, interruption of activities, operational failures, registration errors, misuse of the app, problems with devices, printers, connections, third-party services, or decisions made based on information from the app.
s12.p2: The user acknowledges that they are responsible for validating recorded information, keeping backups, checking amounts, protecting their devices, complying with legal and tax obligations, and using the app appropriately.
s12.p3: Nothing in these Terms of Use excludes rights that cannot be limited under applicable law.

s13.heading: 13. Intellectual property
s13.p1: Ponto Maré, including its name, brand, code, screens, layout, texts, resources, features, design, documentation, and other app elements, belongs to its respective owners and is protected by intellectual property laws.
s13.p2: Use of the app does not grant the user any intellectual property rights, except for the limited license to use provided in these Terms.

s14.heading: 14. Suspension or termination of access
s14.p1: We may suspend, limit, or terminate the user's access to Ponto Maré in case of violation of these Terms of Use, suspected fraud, attempts to bypass payments, misuse, security risk, legal determination, or technical need.
s14.p2: We may also terminate or change features, plans, integrations, or app versions, respecting user rights under applicable law.

s15.heading: 15. Third-party services
s15.p1: Ponto Maré may depend on third-party services such as Supabase, Firebase/Google, Google Play, printing libraries, the operating system, app stores, and other providers.
s15.p2: Use of these services may be subject to the terms, policies, and conditions of the respective third parties.
s15.p3: We are not responsible for failures, unavailability, changes, charges, policies, decisions, restrictions, or data processing performed by third-party services outside our control.

s16.heading: 16. Changes to these terms
s16.p1: These Terms of Use may be updated periodically to reflect changes in the app, features, commercial rules, legal requirements, or operational practices.
s16.p2: When relevant changes occur, the update date will be changed. Continued use of the app after changes indicates agreement with the updated terms.

s17.heading: 17. Applicable law and dispute resolution
s17.p1: These Terms of Use are governed by the laws of the Federative Republic of Brazil.
s17.p2: Any disputes should preferably be resolved by direct contact using the email provided in these Terms. If an amicable solution is not possible, the applicable legal rules will be observed.

s18.heading: 18. Contact
s18.intro: For questions about these Terms of Use, contact us:

pontoMareTerms.footerHtml: Home · Ponto Maré documentation · Privacy policy
